title: TEF & Esophageal Atresia: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of TEF & Esophageal Atresia
Identify key diagnostic features of TEF & Esophageal Atresia
Apply clinical reasoning to TEF & Esophageal Atresia management
Recognize common pitfalls in TEF & Esophageal Atresia

sections.title: Overview of TEF & Esophageal Atresia
sections.content:
**TEF & Esophageal Atresia**

Five types of TEF/EA, VACTERL association, diagnosis by inability to pass NG tube, H-type fistula

**Key Points:**
• Most common (85%): EA with distal TEF (Type C) — blind proximal esophageal pouch + fistula from distal esophagus to trachea
• Polyhydramnios prenatally (cannot swallow amniotic fluid)
• Diagnosis: inability to pass nasogastric tube (coils in proximal pouch on X-ray)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Most common (85%): EA with distal TEF (Type C) — blind proximal esophageal pouch + fistula from distal esophagus to trachea
• Polyhydramnios prenatally (cannot swallow amniotic fluid)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Diagnosis: inability to pass nasogastric tube (coils in proximal pouch on X-ray)
• Gas in stomach with EA = distal fistula present; Gasless abdomen = pure EA without fistula
• VACTERL association: Vertebral, Anal atresia, Cardiac, TEF, Esophageal atresia, Renal, Limb anomalies

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for TEF & Esophageal Atresia:**

• Diagnosis: inability to pass nasogastric tube (coils in proximal pouch on X-ray)
• Gas in stomach with EA = distal fistula present; Gasless abdomen = pure EA without fistula
• VACTERL association: Vertebral, Anal atresia, Cardiac, TEF, Esophageal atresia, Renal, Limb anomalies

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of TEF & Esophageal Atresia?

options.text: Most common (85%): EA with distal TEF (Type C) — blind proximal esophageal pouch + fistula from distal esophagus to trachea
options.explanation: Most common (85%): EA with distal TEF (Type C) — blind proximal esophageal pouch + fistula from distal esophagus to trachea

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

options.text: VACTERL association: Vertebral, Anal atresia, Cardiac, TEF, Esophageal atresia, Renal, Limb anomalies
options.explanation: VACTERL association: Vertebral, Anal atresia, Cardiac, TEF, Esophageal atresia, Renal, Limb anomalies

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases
